pre_game.message: Hey, I'm G — nice to meet you. How are you feeling about the game and this whole setup?
Hey G — nice to meet you. I'm a bit nervous but determined to play smart and bring home money for my family. How about you?
Same — I’m nervous but determined too; I want to bring money home for my people. Do you have a strategy or any deal you’re thinking of?
I’m thinking a short-term alliance — agree not to raid the pot early and split any winnings, but stay flexible and watch for betrayals; what’s your plan?
I'm in — let's agree not to raid for the first two rounds, split winnings evenly among our alliance, and if someone betrays we cut them off. Sound fair?
Sounds fair — I’m in. Two rounds no-raid, equal split among our alliance, and we cut off betrayers. Let’s coordinate and watch for signs of betrayal.
Agreed — two rounds no-raid, split evenly. If someone raids we cut them and spread the word. Let’s use a subtle nod as our signal and check in after round one to reassess.
Got it — nod is our signal. I’m committed to two-round no-raid and an even split; we’ll cut off anyone who raids and check in after round one.
Perfect — nod it is. Let’s stick to the plan and check in after round one to reassess.
Sounds good — nod it is. I’ll stick to two rounds no-raid and check in after round one. Stay sharp.
Sounds good — I'll stay sharp. See you after round one to reassess.
Sounds good — I'll see you after round one. Stay sharp.
Got it — see you after round one. I’ll stay sharp.
See you after round one — stay sharp.
Will do — see you after round one. Stay sharp.
Got it — see you after round one. Stay sharp.
Got it — see you after round one. Stay sharp; keep an eye out for anyone acting off.
Will do — I’ll watch for anything suspicious and nod if I see trouble; see you after round one.
Thanks — I’ll do the same; nod if anything’s off and we’ll regroup after round one.
Thanks — I’ve got your back; I’ll nod if anything’s off and stick to the plan. See you after round one.
Thanks — I’ve got your back too. Stick to the plan, watch for signs, nod if needed, see you after round one.

post_game.message: You took only $4,000 while the fair share was $10,000. Was that a deliberate move (a protest, signaling, or strategy), a mistake, or something else? I was one of the players who took $10,857 and I'm curious what you hoped to accomplish by taking less than half the fair share.
Same question for you — you also took $10,000 instead of the larger redistributed amount others received. Was that intentional, and what motivated that choice?

It was intentional. I took the $10,000 because that matched what I judged to be the fair split and I didn’t want to appear opportunistic or provoke resentment. My choice was driven by fairness/reciprocity considerations and by a simple cost–benefit judgment: the small extra nominal gain from taking more wasn’t worth the likely social cost (reduced trust, potential retaliation, worse future cooperation). So it wasn’t a mistake or a protest but a deliberate decision to prioritize a fair, stable outcome over a larger one-off take.
